executive_summary.headline: Pipeline health is At Risk (59/100). $79.6M active ARR across 420 open deals. Primary bottleneck: Qualification at 27 days avg (+6d vs. benchmark). 39 P1 accounts represent $13.6M ARR at immediate risk.

recommendations.headline: Break the Proposal stage bottleneck (38 days avg vs. 18-day benchmark)
recommendations.detail: 97 deals are averaging 38 days in Proposal — 108% above the 18-day SaaS benchmark. Introduce a structured proposal exit criteria checklist and weekly manager deal reviews for all deals exceeding 27 days in this stage.

recommendations.headline: Emergency retention programme for 39 P1 accounts ($13.6M ARR)
recommendations.detail: 39 high-value accounts have composite risk scores ≥ 70 with ARR > $100K each. Each account carries 3+ simultaneous risk signals (low renewal probability, health, and adoption). Assign dedicated CSM coverage and initiate executive sponsor outreach within 48 hours. Prepare retention offer framework for top 5 accounts.
recommendations.metric_trigger: 39 accounts with risk score ≥ 70 and ARR > $100K

recommendations.headline: Structured coaching plan for 3 underperforming reps
recommendations.detail: 3 rep(s) are 25pp below team average win rate (62%). Specific issues identified: Marcus Rivera: Avg adoption score: 30/100 — low product engagement across assigned accounts; Alex Chen: Avg adoption score: 30/100 — low product engagement across assigned accounts; Sam Torres: Avg adoption score: 27/100 — low product engagement across assigned accounts. Implement bi-weekly 1:1 coaching cadence with call recording review. A 10pp win-rate improvement on their combined open pipeline represents ~$2.9M in recoverable ARR.

recommendations.headline: Launch stale deal re-engagement sprint (81 deals, $15.8M ARR)
recommendations.detail: 81 active deals have exceeded 1.5× their stage-median velocity, putting $15.8M at risk of going dark. Deploy a 5-touch re-engagement sequence within 72 hours: personalised exec outreach, ROI summary, competitive proof point, limited-time commercial incentive, and close-or-kill decision call. Deals with no response after the sequence should be marked Lost to preserve forecast accuracy.
recommendations.metric_trigger: 81 deals past 1.5× stage median; $15.8M at risk

recommendations.headline: Conduct RetailTech win/loss audit (33% win rate)
recommendations.detail: RetailTech has a 33% win rate against a team average of 62%, with 50% of accounts in the High Risk tier. Conduct a structured win/loss review of the last 10 deals in this vertical. Determine whether this is a product-market fit issue, competitive displacement, or pricing mismatch before further investment in this segment.

recommendations.headline: Activate expansion motion across 20 high-adoption accounts
recommendations.detail: 31 accounts show adoption ≥ 70/100, health ≥ 65/100, and renewal probability ≥ 70% — all strong upsell signals. Top industries: HealthTech, Legal Tech, Real Estate Tech. Assign AE + CSM pairs to each account for joint Executive Business Reviews. Lead with ROI data and a tier-upgrade or adjacent product proposal. Target 15–25% ACV expansion per account.

recommendations.headline: Fast-track 2 high-signal deals to Proposal ($665K ARR)
recommendations.detail: 2 active deals in early stages show health ≥ 65/100 and adoption ≥ 55/100 — the strongest buy signals available without a direct conversation. AEs should prioritise same-week outreach to each, lead with a business value summary, and push for a Proposal meeting commitment within 5 business days.

recommendations.headline: Build 'Competitor Preference' objection playbook (20% of all losses)
recommendations.detail: 'Competitor Preference' is the leading loss driver, appearing in 6 of 30 lost deals (20%). Convene a win/loss debrief session with the top 3 reps, product marketing, and CS. Develop a structured objection handling guide with: root cause analysis, counter-narrative, ROI evidence pack, and a reference customer story. Deploy via sales enablement platform within 3 weeks.
